title: KubeVirt VM Not Starting / VirtualMachine in Stopped or Error State
symptoms: kubectl get vm shows VM in Stopped or ErrorUnschedulable state
kubectl get vmi shows no VMI object or VMI in Failed state
VM pod (virt-launcher-*) in Pending or Error state
causes: Insufficient node resources (CPU, memory) for VM request
KVM kernel module not available on node (virtualization not enabled)
virt-launcher image pull failure
PVC referenced by VM not bound
Node selector / affinity rules preventing scheduling
virt-handler pod not running on the target node
remediation: If node lacks /dev/kvm: enable nested virtualization on the hypervisor or use hardware that supports KVM
If resource constrained: check node capacity, scale cluster or resize VM
If PVC not bound: fix the underlying storage issue first (see Rook-Ceph runbooks)
Restart virt-operator: kubectl -n kubevirt rollout restart deploy/virt-operator
Restart virt-controller: kubectl -n kubevirt rollout restart deploy/virt-controller
Force VM restart: kubectl patch vm <name> -n <ns> --type merge -p '{"spec":{"running":false}}' then set running: true
Check virt-handler on target node: kubectl -n kubevirt get pod -l kubevirt.io=virt-handler -o wide
escalation: If KVM is not available cluster-wide, no VMs can run. This is a hardware/configuration issue requiring platform team involvement.

title: KubeVirt virt-launcher Pod CrashLoopBackOff
symptoms: VM exists but VMI shows Running then immediately stops
kubectl logs virt-launcher shows QEMU/KVM crash
causes: QEMU crash due to guest OS kernel panic
Memory overcommit causing OOMKill of virt-launcher
Disk I/O error on backing PVC (Ceph issue)
virt-launcher image incompatible with node kernel
CPU feature compatibility issue between host and guest
remediation: For OOMKill: increase VM memory in VirtualMachine spec and restart
For disk I/O error: check Rook-Ceph cluster health, repair OSDs
For QEMU crash: check VM serial console log, may need guest OS investigation
Restart VM: kubectl patch vm <name> -n <ns> --type merge -p '{"spec":{"running":false}}' then true
If image incompatible: update KubeVirt version: kubectl -n kubevirt edit kubevirt kubevirt
escalation: Persistent QEMU crashes may indicate hardware issues. Escalate if crash recurs after restart without load.

title: KubeVirt Live Migration Failing
symptoms: kubectl get virtualmachineinstancemigration shows Failing state
VM migration stuck in Scheduling or Preparing state
Node maintenance cannot complete because VM won't migrate
causes: Target node has insufficient resources
Network bandwidth too low for migration (large RAM VMs)
Migration timeout exceeded (default: 150s per GiB)
VirtualMachineInstance has migration-blocking devices (passthrough GPU, host disk)
virt-handler on target node not reachable
investigation: kubectl -n kubevirt logs deploy/virt-controller | grep migration
kubectl -n kubevirt get kubevirt kubevirt -o yaml | grep -A10 migrations
remediation: Cancel stuck migration: kubectl delete vmim <name> -n <namespace>
Check target node resources: kubectl describe node <target>
Increase migration timeout: edit KubeVirt CR spec.configuration.migrations.completionTimeoutPerGiB
For bandwidth: increase parallelMigrationsPerCluster limit or migrate during low-traffic window
If node draining: force evict the VM pod only as last resort (VM will restart cold)
escalation: If node cannot be drained for maintenance due to failed migrations, escalate to schedule downtime window.

title: KubeVirt Components Down / virt-operator Not Running
symptoms: kubectl -n kubevirt get pods shows virt-operator or virt-api in Error/CrashLoop
Cannot create or modify VirtualMachine objects
kubectl get vmi fails or VMs stuck in Pending state
causes: KubeVirt upgrade in progress or failed
CRD version mismatch after cluster upgrade
virt-operator image pull failure
RBAC permissions revoked
investigation: kubectl get crd virtualmachines.kubevirt.io -o yaml | grep -i version
remediation: Restart virt-operator: kubectl -n kubevirt rollout restart deploy/virt-operator
Check KubeVirt CR status: kubectl -n kubevirt describe kubevirt kubevirt
If upgrade stuck: kubectl -n kubevirt patch kubevirt kubevirt --type merge -p '{"spec":{"imageTag":"<version>"}}'
Verify RBAC: kubectl get clusterrolebinding | grep kubevirt
Full reinstall only as last resort: preserve VMs by backing up VM CRs first
escalation: KubeVirt control plane outage prevents all VM operations. Existing running VMs continue to run but cannot be managed.
